topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — c7 adversarial review of EXP-194 sub-terrestrial-return-channel preemption paragraph
change_summary: EXP-194 integration (commit b4fa494) inserted a new ~313-word paragraph into detail_evidence between the 1,600:1 flux-conservation paragraph and the 'second self-contradiction' paragraph. The new paragraph preempts the advocate-mode 'narrow sub-terrestrial return channel' defense with a forced disjunction: (1) if 64,852 nT is a surface prediction, the 1,600:1 flux contradiction stands; (2) if it's sub-terranean, the dome needs globe-style crustal propagation physics to bridge to the observed ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole, which collapses the architecture into a relabelled geodynamo. c7 attacks the new paragraph for factual accuracy, logical completeness, and internal consistency with the surrounding text.
our_argument_summary: WIN-053 is Self-Contradicted. The new EXP-194 paragraph closes the 'narrow return channel' advocate-mode defense by forcing a two-horn disjunction — either the 64,852 nT southern coefficient is a surface prediction (in which case flux conservation in the dome's claimed ring-magnet geometry predicts B_south ~39 nT against the fitted 64,852 nT, a 1,660:1 internal contradiction), or it is a sub-terranean tube value (in which case the dome's own surface-data RMS validation no longer supports the 20% improvement claim, AND the dome silently imports globe-style crustal propagation physics to bridge sub-terranean flux up to surface observations — re-relabelling the geodynamo rather than replacing it). The paragraph is structurally sound and closes the specific defense it targets; the remaining weaknesses are internal-consistency and precision issues in how the forcing move is executed.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-053 remains static-prose-only; raw-text contains no monitor.py domain for B(r), no 62,376 / 64,852 / 8619 references inside a B(r) context, no 'Closed Toroidal Ovoid' / 'ring magnet' / 'Sump' / 'Axis Mundi' tokens, and no '61% -> 20% RMS' statement inside the archived raw-text. The 8,619 km scale length appears only in H(r) = 8537*exp(-r/8619) km contexts (raw-text/01-home.txt line 28, raw-text/05-model.txt line 33). The Closed Toroidal Ovoid / B(r) claim therefore appears to be sourced from the V51.0/V51.1 predictions page that is not fully captured in the 2026-04-07 raw-text archive.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static prose; no live monitoring or comparison against observed magnetic data. Consistent with prior cycles. The paragraph EXP-194 adds does not introduce any new code or data-fetch dependency.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c6: the +/-15% falsification threshold on lambda = 8,619 km is loose enough that any future fit converging near lambda cleanly 'passes,' and one outside it would still be ~1% of the 1,600:1 contradiction we already show.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. c7 re-ran grep on raw-text/*.txt for INTERMAGNET, CHAOS-7, 'Closed Toroidal', 'ring magnet', '61%', '20% RMS', '62376', '64852'. INTERMAGNET and CHAOS-7 appear as generic dome-validated data sources in raw-text/01-home.txt line 10 ('independent observational data: INTERMAGNET magnetic observatories, CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic records, ...') but are NOT specifically tied to a B(r) / 20% RMS calculation in the archived raw-text. See H2 below.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: grep on raw-text/*.txt confirms no monitor.py domain touches WIN-053. The 8,619 km scale length is reused from H(r) (firmament height) without an independent B(r) derivation. WIN-053 does not appear in the live dashboard's 20-domain table (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 61-130 lists the 20 Domains — SAA Decay, NMP Drift, etc. — but no 'B(r) pole formula' domain). Consistent with c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c6. Geomagnetic field structure is predicted by IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 / WMM via degree-13 spherical harmonics grounded in geodynamo physics at ~50 nT global RMS; the dome's ~20% RMS (~10,000-13,000 nT error) is 200x worse. The dome renames the mechanism (aetheric toroidal flow) but cannot reproduce localized anomalies (SAA shape) or secular variation that the standard model handles routinely.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c6. A=62,376 nT and C=64,852 nT align with observed pole magnitudes (NMP ~54-56k; SMP ~65-67k per WMM2025) published for decades before V51. PRED-TOROID-002 was filed 2026-04-04 with a claimed confirmation date of 2026-03-23 — the confirmation predates registration, making this a retrodiction registered after the fact.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c6. No formula derives A / C from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any other dome parameter. Only lambda = 8,619 km is shared between H(r) and B(r), which is cross-domain parameter reuse rather than a derivation. The exponential form is also not derived from any toroidal topology — a tightly-wound solenoidal toroid gives B ~ 1/r, not e^(-r/lambda).

holes_found.description: INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY on the southern comparator: the new EXP-194 paragraph (now paragraph 2 of detail_evidence) pins the dome's B(r) validation to '~55,000 nT actually measured at the geographic south pole' and builds the crustal-propagation horn of the disjunction on the 64,852 -> 55,000 gap. But paragraph 3 (the coefficient-circularity paragraph, unchanged by EXP-194) still says the coefficients 'closely match observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~54,000-56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~65,000-67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole per WMM2025.' A careful defender sees two different comparators for the same coefficient: paragraph 2 uses the geographic south pole (~55k), paragraph 3 uses the Magnetic South Pole (~65k). These anchor to different physical locations — NMP/SMP are not at the geographic poles (SMP is at approximately 64 deg S, 138 deg E). Under the dome's radial geometry the correct comparator for r_S=0 is the disc rim = geographic south pole (~55k), which is paragraph 2's choice. Paragraph 3's SMP comparator is geographically wrong for a dome-native radial model, and the apparent 'close match' (64,852 vs 65-67k) is an artifact of comparing to the wrong location. A defender who reads paragraph 3 first will conclude '64,852 matches 65,000 fine — no propagation gap, paragraph 2 is chasing a phantom.' A defender who reads paragraph 2 first will conclude '55,000 target, 64,852 coefficient — but paragraph 3 says 65,000, so which is it?' The new paragraph is correct on its own terms; paragraph 3 is the one now exposed as using a geographically-wrong comparator.
holes_found.recommendation: Option A (preferred): update paragraph 3 to align with paragraph 2's geographic-pole framing. Specifically, replace 'closely match observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~54,000-56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~65,000-67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole per WMM2025' with 'closely match observed magnetic-pole-region field strengths, even though under the dome's own radial geometry the relevant comparators are the geographic poles at the Axis Mundi and the disc rim — where IGRF-13 reports ~55,000 nT at both ends. A = 62,376 nT and C = 64,852 nT are therefore ~13% and ~18% above the observationally-correct on-dome comparators.' This makes paragraph 3 consistent with paragraph 2 AND strengthens the 'fitted to observed' argument by showing the coefficients are fitted to the Magnetic-Pole values rather than the dome-correct geographic-pole values — another relabelling. Option B (lighter edit): add a parenthetical to paragraph 2 acknowledging the MSP comparator paragraph 3 uses and explaining why the geographic-south-pole number is the correct one under dome radial geometry. Either fix closes the inconsistency; Option A is preferred because it tightens paragraph 3 on its merits.

holes_found.description: 'EXPLICITLY INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7' OVERCLAIM. The new paragraph says 'the dome's own 61% -> 20% RMS improvement is computed against surface-observatory data — explicitly the INTERMAGNET ground-station network and the CHAOS-7 satellite-and-observatory composite.' The word 'explicitly' attributes a specific per-WIN dataset choice to the dome. What the dome actually says in raw-text/01-home.txt line 10 is a GLOBAL validation statement: 'The Dome Cosmological Model ... tests [predictions] against independent observational data: INTERMAGNET magnetic observatories, CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic records, NOAA magnetic pole tracking, eclipse gravity measurements, Schumann resonance data, and direct astronomical observation.' This lists INTERMAGNET and CHAOS-7 among the dome's data sources overall, but does NOT specifically tie the 20% RMS figure for WIN-053 to those two datasets. A careful defender will object: 'You cherry-picked two data sources from a six-item home-page list and attributed a per-WIN dataset choice we never made. We could equally have used NOAA magnetic pole tracking or ground-survey data for the 20% RMS computation.' The 'explicitly' makes the surface-reading horn of the disjunction feel forced — a weaker adverb would survive that objection while preserving the argument. A secondary issue: the dome describes CHAOS-7 as 'paleomagnetic records,' which is factually wrong (CHAOS-7 is a present-day geomagnetic model derived from Swarm / CHAMP / observatories, not paleomagnetic). Our paragraph correctly characterizes CHAOS-7 as 'satellite-and-observatory composite,' which creates a second asymmetry: we are citing the dome's dataset list while simultaneously correcting the dome's characterization of one of the items in that list. That asymmetry is fine and even useful, but should be acknowledged rather than smoothed over.
holes_found.recommendation: Weaken 'explicitly' to 'the surface-observatory sources the dome's own home page highlights (INTERMAGNET ground stations and CHAOS-7, which the dome mischaracterizes as "paleomagnetic" but which is actually a present-day satellite-and-observatory composite).' This (a) drops the 'explicitly' overclaim by making the attribution a general characterization rather than a per-WIN dataset commitment, (b) preempts the 'we never specified that dataset for this WIN' defense by noting the dome's generic list, and (c) turns the dome's own mischaracterization of CHAOS-7 into an additional credibility signal at zero argumentative cost. The surface-reading horn still lands — INTERMAGNET is indisputably a surface network, and the dome has told the world it validates against surface data.

holes_found.description: FORCED DISJUNCTION MISSES THE METAPHYSICAL RETREAT. The paragraph presents exactly two horns: (1) surface-prediction = 1,600:1 contradiction stands, or (2) sub-terranean-prediction = architecture collapses into a relabelled geodynamo. But EXP-194's own anticipated_objections explicitly considers a third path: the defender retreats to 'B(r) is a phenomenological surface-fit equation while the toroidal geometry is metaphysical, so flux conservation need not apply at the surface.' EXP-194 correctly notes this move 'concedes the central WIN-053 claim' — but the paragraph as written does NOT put that third horn in front of the reader. A sophisticated dome defender will pick up the paragraph, see the two horns, and immediately say 'neither — we pick Option 3: the toroidal flow is aetheric, not Maxwellian; Maxwell's ∇·B = 0 does not apply to aetheric flow; B(r) is just an empirical fit to surface data with no ring-magnet commitment; flux conservation is simply inapplicable.' Our current paragraph has no on-page rebuttal to that move. Yes, it concedes the WIN-053 claim (geometry no longer predicts the dipole) and makes A, C, lambda fitted parameters — but the reader has to work that out for themselves, which a defender will not do for us.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a one-sentence third-horn closure after the existing two-horn disjunction. Proposed text (fits naturally after 'only a relabelling of it'): 'A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian and that ∇·B = 0 therefore does not apply — abandons the very claim WIN-053 advertises (the toroidal geometry predicts a dipolar field) and demotes B(r) to a parameterless surface fit with no derivation from dome geometry, putting WIN-053 squarely into the "observed values relabeled" category documented in Section 6.1.' This closes the metaphysical-retreat loophole explicitly, credits the defender's strongest available move, and folds it back into the §6.1 argument already in hand.

holes_found.description: 'MULTI-LAYER CRUSTAL MAGNETIC CONDUCTION WITH DEPTH-DEPENDENT SUSCEPTIBILITY' is technically imprecise as a label for how standard geomagnetism bridges deep sources to surface fields. Standard geomagnetism handles the deep-source-to-surface problem primarily via (a) spherical harmonic decomposition of the observed surface field, (b) potential-field continuation (Laplace's equation for ∇ x B = 0 in source-free regions above the core-mantle boundary), and (c) a separate shallow crustal anomaly model (from aeromagnetic surveys) fit on top of the core field. 'Magnetic conduction' more properly describes induced-current EM behaviour in geodynamo-relevant conductivity studies (the conductivity layering between core, mantle, crust) — which is relevant for secular variation and induction, not for the steady-state pole-to-surface propagation the paragraph invokes. The current label isn't wrong enough to hand the dome a reply — standard geomag absolutely does use susceptibility and layered models in the crustal-anomaly fit — but the label as written conflates three things (core-field continuation, crustal susceptibility, EM induction) a geomag specialist would keep separate.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the parenthetical. Suggested: '...a physical mechanism by which a narrow sub-crustal flux of order 65,000 nT propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust to produce the ~55,000 nT actually measured at the geographic south pole. That mechanism — potential-field continuation of a deep source combined with a crustal-anomaly susceptibility model — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics...' Replaces the slightly-wrong 'multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction' with the more standard 'potential-field continuation of a deep source combined with a crustal-anomaly susceptibility model.' Optional polish; argument stands either way.

holes_found.description: 'THOUSANDS OF KILOMETRES OF ROCK' may read as a crust-only depth claim. Continental crust is ~35 km; oceanic crust ~7 km. 'Thousands of km' of rock is the mantle (to ~2,900 km) and the dome's own Sub-H(r) which extends to ~6,371 km (Earth radius). Our paragraph calls this 'sub-crustal' in the next clause ('a narrow sub-crustal flux'), which is correct — but within that clause the phrase 'thousands of kilometres of rock' without the 'mantle' qualifier could let a careless reader think we're claiming crustal thicknesses on that scale. This is minor because the very next sentence references 'multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction' (itself flagged in the previous finding), which is part of the same conflation.
holes_found.recommendation: Single-word polish: change 'propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of rock' to 'propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust' — accurate and makes the propagation depth scale explicit. Matches the dome's own Sub-H(r) = H(r) x (1 - exp(-r/6371 km)) depth scale in detail_extra.

holes_found.description: The new paragraph does not include a cross-reference to Section 6.1 ('Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters'), even though both horns of the disjunction re-surface the §6.1 argument (the surface horn leaves A, C, lambda as fitted parameters; the sub-terranean horn admits a phenomenological surface fit is what actually validates the 20% RMS, which IS a hidden fit). The tldr_verdict already cross-refs §6.1 clickably (as of the c6 patch). Adding one inline reference at the end of paragraph 2 would tie the advocate-mode closure back to the already-existing structural critique. Minor and optional — the argument works without the cross-ref — but it's consistent with the rest of the detail_evidence pattern which links to §6.4 and WIN-012 at the end of paragraph 3.
holes_found.recommendation: Append to the final sentence of the new paragraph: '...only a relabelling of it (see §6.1 on hidden fitted parameters).' Single-anchor addition; matches the cross-ref style already used in the tldr_verdict. If combined with the H3 third-horn fix, the §6.1 anchor should land inside the new third-horn sentence rather than the current closer. Low priority.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1243 (c5 H3): the specific 'symmetric Sump aperture' rescue (A = C by construction in a symmetric circuit) is distinct from the 'narrow channel flux preservation' rescue EXP-194 just closed. The symmetric-aperture rescue says the two apertures are identical in area and that's why A ≈ C. Our rebuttal (A and C differ by 4% and both match asymmetric WMM pole strengths) is not captured by the new EXP-194 paragraph, which addresses only the narrow-channel rescue. c7 confirms ISS-1243 remains open and unaddressed by this expansion.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; ISS-1243 is already tracked and will be addressed by a future expansion.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1244 (c5 H6): the 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row is still absent from the sensitivity parenthetical (current text has 500 km and 1,000 km only). At 3,000 km aperture the ratio is still ~45:1 and the discrepancy still ~46×, which is a striking additional row. c7 confirms ISS-1244 remains open.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; ISS-1244 is already tracked.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1245 (c5 H7): the source-vs-sink polarity finding (the dome's B(r) is A*exp + C*exp with both positive-signed, topologically a monopole pair not a source-sink dipole) is still absent from detail_evidence. c7 confirms ISS-1245 remains open.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; ISS-1245 is already tracked.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1246 (c5 H8): Sub-H(r) delta = 6,371 km is still unsourced in detail_extra — no dome-site URL or in-text citation. c7 confirms ISS-1246 remains open.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; ISS-1246 is already tracked.

stronger_arguments.description: ADVOCATE-MODE: THE 20% RMS NUMBER IS NEVER SOURCED. EXP-194 argues that if the coefficient is sub-terranean, the 20% RMS claim 'evaporates' because RMS was computed against surface data. This is rhetorically effective but rests on the assumption that the 20% RMS WAS computed against surface data — which the dome has never explicitly stated per-WIN. A stronger move is to flip the burden: the dome claims a 20% RMS improvement but provides no stated dataset, no computation, no residual plot, and no per-latitude error table anywhere we have found in raw-text or the current predictions page. Before we argue about whether that 20% 'evaporates' under re-interpretation, we could point out the dome has never substantiated it in the first place. 'The 20% figure is an unsourced literary claim; the dome has never published the dataset, the RMS formula, or a residual map that would let an independent reader reproduce it.' This is a preceding argument to the disjunction — if the 20% is unsourced, neither horn of the disjunction is needed to defeat it. Keeping it as a fallback argument strengthens WIN-053 against a defender who says 'the 20% is fine, our validation stands.'
sources: raw-text/*.txt (no 20% RMS or 61% RMS magnetic figures; raw-text/01-home.txt line 48 references a 48.6% RMS reduction but that is WIN-030 ellipse geometry, not WIN-053 magnetic field)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (20-domain table, V51.0, shows no 'B(r) pole formula' domain with an RMS metric)
raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 61 ('WIN-030 (ellipse) — disc is not circular, 5-48% RMS improvement') — the only RMS-improvement claim sourced in raw-text is ellipse geometry, not magnetic

stronger_arguments.description: CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY — THE MAGNETIC-VS-GEOGRAPHIC POLE SLIP REAPPEARS IN WIN-037 / WIN-035. The new paragraph correctly uses the geographic south pole comparator (~55,000 nT). This exposes a small pattern: several WINs in the geomagnetic family (WIN-035 SAA, WIN-036 NMP longitude, WIN-037 SAA decay, WIN-010 eclipse magnetic anomaly) all cite Magnetic-Pole-anchored data (NOAA WMM NMP / INTERMAGNET SAA region). For radially-symmetric dome claims anchored at the Axis Mundi, those WINs face the same comparator question: is the dome's r = 0 the geographic pole or the magnetic pole? A coherent cross-WIN reading requires a single answer. This is out of scope for WIN-053's paragraph 2 fix, but the analyst / decider should know that Option A of the H1 fix (updating paragraph 3 to use geographic-pole comparators) will have cross-WIN ripple effects worth auditing.
sources: wins.json WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037 detail_evidence fields
raw-text/02-wins.txt lines 37-45 (W009, W010, W011 NOAA WMM2025 references)

stronger_arguments.description: QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION — THE CRUSTAL-PROPAGATION LOSS ESTIMATE. The paragraph asserts 64,852 nT sub-terranean -> ~55,000 nT surface requires 'crustal conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility' but does not quantify the implied attenuation. Rough estimate: 64,852 / 55,000 ≈ 1.18, so ~18% attenuation across ~6,371 km of rock (using the dome's own Sub-H = 6,371 km scale). Standard crustal susceptibility is chi ≈ 10^-4 to 10^-2 (SI) — the induced field is a small fraction of the applied field, so 18% attenuation through 6,000+ km of mantle+crust is implausibly LITTLE. Either the dome's 64,852 nT sub-terranean source is mostly traveling as free-space field (not attenuated, a classic potential-field continuation from a deep source — which is exactly the geodynamo solution), OR the attenuation is stronger than observed and the source field must be much larger than 64,852 nT to produce 55,000 nT at the surface (which would imply the dome's B(r) equation is not the actual source magnitude, further disconnecting the fitted coefficients from any physical claim). A quantitative sentence here would turn the qualitative 'imports globe-style physics' into a concrete 'either free-space continuation (geodynamo relabelled) or attenuation too strong for crustal susceptibility (coefficients are not the source field).' Optional strengthening; would expand the paragraph by ~30 words.
sources: Standard geomagnetism: crustal susceptibility chi ~ 10^-4 to 10^-2 SI (Kono M., 'Geomagnetism: An Introduction and Overview,' Treatise on Geophysics v.5, 2015, §5.1)

Potential-field continuation: the surface magnetic field above a deep source in a source-free region is given by B_surface = (R_source/R_surface)^(n+2) * B_source for spherical harmonic degree n — which for n=1 dipole gives ~8% reduction from core to surface, consistent with the classical core-to-surface attenuation that IGRF/CHAOS handle via spherical harmonic degree-based scaling.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-194 correctly identifies that our pre-integration detail_evidence had a real weakness: the 1,600:1 ratio depends on the return surface spanning the full disc, and a defender could legitimately argue the dome never stipulated that. The new paragraph handles this well — it acknowledges the charitable reading explicitly, then closes the door via two observations (surface-data-validation pins the coefficient as a surface value; crustal-propagation requires globe-style physics). The forced disjunction is the right analytical move: a defender who accepts either horn loses the WIN-053 claim.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The disjunction is sound, but the execution has the three issues above (internal inconsistency with paragraph 3's MSP comparator, 'explicitly INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7' overclaim, missing metaphysical-retreat third horn). None of them reverse the verdict — WIN-053 is still Self-Contradicted — but they leave seams a careful defender can widen. The c7 recommendations address those seams directly without weakening the paragraph's argumentative core.

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-194 paragraph (commit b4fa494) does what it was designed to do — it closes the c3/c4-identified 'narrow sub-terrestrial return channel' advocate-mode rescue by pinning the 64,852 nT southern coefficient as a surface value (via the dome's own surface-data validation) OR forcing the dome into a crustal-propagation argument that silently imports the geodynamo. The paragraph's internal structure is good: acknowledge the defender's move charitably, then offer two closing observations that together forbid both an exit.

Three issues remain.

(1) Internal inconsistency with paragraph 3. The new paragraph 2 uses '~55,000 nT actually measured at the geographic south pole' as its propagation target. Paragraph 3, unchanged by EXP-194, still compares coefficients to 'observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~54,000-56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~65,000-67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole per WMM2025.' These are two different physical locations: NMP and SMP are not at the geographic poles (SMP is at ~64 deg S, ~138 deg E in 2025). The dome's B(r) is radially symmetric from the Axis Mundi, so under dome geometry r_S = 0 corresponds to the disc rim = geographic south pole, and the correct comparator for C = 64,852 nT is the field at the geographic south pole (~55,000 nT per IGRF-13). Paragraph 2 has the correct comparator; paragraph 3's MSP/NMP comparator is geographically wrong for a dome-native radial model, and the 'close match' it reports is an artifact of comparing C to the wrong location. A defender who reads paragraph 3 first walks away thinking 64,852 ≈ 65,000 is a close match and there is no propagation gap; a defender who reads paragraph 2 first notices the tension and asks which comparator is correct. Option A (preferred fix): rewrite paragraph 3 to use geographic-pole comparators, which makes both paragraphs consistent AND strengthens the 'fitted to observed' argument (fitted to Magnetic-Pole values even though dome geometry demands geographic-pole values — itself a relabelling). Option B: add a reconciling parenthetical to paragraph 2. Either closes the inconsistency.

(2) 'Explicitly INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7' is a per-WIN attribution we don't have. The dome's home page lists INTERMAGNET and CHAOS-7 as global dome-validated data sources (alongside NOAA magnetic pole tracking, eclipse gravity, Schumann data, and astronomical observation), but does not specifically say the 20% RMS figure for the Closed Toroidal Ovoid was computed against INTERMAGNET + CHAOS-7. Saying 'explicitly' overclaims and hands a defender the rejoinder 'we never committed to that dataset for this WIN.' A weaker adverb ('the surface-observatory sources the dome's own home page highlights' instead of 'explicitly INTERMAGNET... and CHAOS-7...') survives that objection while preserving the surface-reading force. Bonus: the dome describes CHAOS-7 as 'paleomagnetic records' (raw-text/01-home.txt line 10), which is wrong — CHAOS-7 is a present-day Swarm/CHAMP/observatory composite, not paleomagnetic. The current paragraph correctly characterizes CHAOS-7 as 'satellite-and-observatory composite,' creating an implicit correction that could be made explicit to zero cost.

(3) The forced disjunction is two-horned, but there's a third horn EXP-194's own anticipated_objections acknowledges. A defender's strongest escape from the two stated horns is to retreat to a metaphysical reading: 'B(r) is a phenomenological surface fit, the toroidal flow is aetheric (not Maxwellian), ∇·B = 0 does not apply to aetheric flow, so flux conservation is inapplicable.' EXP-194 correctly notes that this move 'concedes the central WIN-053 claim.' But the paragraph as written does not put that third horn in front of the reader. A one-sentence third-horn closure would reuse the §6.1 'Zero Fitted Parameters' architecture already in hand and leave no defender-usable exit.

The two minor-severity issues (crustal-magnetic-conduction label technical imprecision; 'thousands of kilometres of rock' without the 'mantle' qualifier) are polish. Four open c5 issues (ISS-1243-1246) remain untouched by this expansion — as expected, since EXP-194 was scoped narrowly to ISS-1225 only (c7 re-confirms the four are still open and unresolved).

Advocate-mode stress test: the strongest defender move against the new paragraph IS the metaphysical retreat (third horn). The second-strongest is the 'we never specified that dataset for this WIN' rejoinder to the 'explicitly INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7' line. Both are addressed by the H2 and H3 recommendations.

Cross-WIN consistency: the magnetic-pole vs geographic-pole slip flagged in H1 appears in paragraph 3 and also lurks in WIN-035 / WIN-036 / WIN-037 / WIN-010 where dome claims are anchored against NOAA NMP / INTERMAGNET SAA measurements. The dome's r = 0 for radially-symmetric claims should be the geographic pole (the Axis Mundi) under any consistent reading of dome geometry. A cross-WIN audit after Option A would close the pattern.

Quantitative verification: the crustal-propagation horn asserts 64,852 nT (sub-terranean) -> ~55,000 nT (surface) requires 'multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction.' The implied attenuation is only ~18%, which is actually consistent with potential-field continuation from a deep source in a source-free region (the classical geodynamo-to-surface solution) — meaning the second horn of the disjunction is tighter than the paragraph itself makes clear. Either the dome's 64,852 nT IS the core-field-equivalent coefficient that standard geomag handles via spherical harmonics (geodynamo relabelled), or the attenuation is much stronger than crustal susceptibility can provide and the dome's 64,852 nT coefficient is not the physical source magnitude (coefficients disconnected from any physical quantity). Optional strengthening noted in stronger_arguments.

Verdict: Self-Contradicted remains correct. current_verdict_holds = true. recommended_action = minor_edit — the new paragraph is structurally sound; the three findings-for-edit are polish that widens the audience a sophisticated dome-defender can reach. None of them would reverse the verdict or the forcing disjunction's conclusion.

citations_verified: https://intermagnet.github.io/ — dome-acknowledged data source per raw-text/01-home.txt line 10; c7 notes the URL is consistent with the official INTERMAGNET project page (not independently HTTP-verified in this sandbox run, but matches the URL pattern and c6 verified the sibling CHAOS-7 URL). Recommend HTTP re-verification on the next curmudgeon run with outbound HTTP.
